title: tetraria pygmaea
intro: Tetraria pygmaea is a small, aquatic plant species belonging to the Restionaceae family, commonly found in wetlands and marshy areas. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a traditional remedy for conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Tetraria pygmaea is often used in the form of infusions or decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional medicines and tonics.
